rationale: det_receive_impl_equal requires post1_self_.receive_state.epmap == post2_self_.receive_state.epmap and the analogous send_state equality. Both .epmap fields are HashMap<V>, which is declared #[verifier(external_body)] with only an uninterp view(). In Verus, == on external_body types is uninterpreted (verified with a minimal test case: a@==b@ does not imply a==b for opaque types). The receive_impl contract constrains only the abstract view via SingleDelivery::receive(pre@, post@, ...), which fixes post1@==post2@ but cannot bridge to the concrete HashMap. No combination of asserts/case-splits over r1/r2 variants or pkt.msg variants closes the gap because the obligation is on concrete external_body values, not their abstract views. Forbidden bypasses (assume/admit/external_body/assume_specification) would be required. Tried: empty body, direct epmap equality asserts, full-struct equality assert (post1_self_==post2_self_), and rebinding helpers. All failed at epmap equality.
